questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: A block of mass 20 kg slides on a rough horizontal plane surface. Let the speed of the block at a particular instant is 10 m/s. It comes to rest after travelling a distance of 20 m. Which one of the following could be the magnitude of the frictional force?
questions.solution:
Let’s use the work–energy principle. The loss in kinetic energy equals the work done by friction:

Initial kinetic energy

$$K_i = \tfrac12 m v^2 = \tfrac12 \times 20\,\text{kg} \times (10\,\text{m/s})^2 = 1000\,\text{J}.$$

Work done by friction

$$W_{\rm fric} = f \times s,$$

where $f$ is the frictional force and $s=20$ m is the stopping distance.

Equate work to energy change

$$f \,s = K_i \quad\Longrightarrow\quad f = \frac{1000\,\text{J}}{20\,\text{m}} = 50\,\text{N}.$$

Answer: 50 N (Option 4).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is the usual unit of measurement for Air Pressure used in India?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option B: Millibar. Here's why:

Air pressure is the weight of the atmosphere pressing down on a given area. It's measured as the force per unit area. Here's a breakdown of why the other options are incorrect and why millibars are used:

  • Centimeter (Option A): Centimeters measure length or distance, not pressure.
  • Milligram (Option C): Milligrams measure mass, not pressure.
  • Milliliter (Option D): Milliliters measure volume, not pressure.
  • Millibar (Option B): Millibars are the standard unit of measurement for air pressure in India and many other parts of the world. A millibar is a unit of pressure equal to 1/1000 of a bar.

In India, weather reports and forecasts typically use millibars (mb) to indicate air pressure. Other units of measurement for air pressure exist (like inches of mercury), but millibars are the most common in India.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: The energy is always conserved for a system which is
questions.solution:
Energy stays the same, or is "conserved," in a system when it doesn’t exchange energy or matter with anything outside of it.

An "isolated system" is a system where this no-exchange condition is met.

In an isolated system, the total energy doesn’t change over time, which means energy conservation is always present.

For systems that are "non-isolated," energy can move in and out, so energy conservation isn't guaranteed for just that system.

Therefore, the correct answer is Option 1: Isolated only.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: <!-- x-tinymce/html -->An astronaut whose weight on the Earth is 600 N experiences weightless- ness on International Space Station orbiting around the Earth. It means that
options: normal reaction of the space- station floor on the astronaut is zero
gravitational pull of earth on the astronaut is zero
space station applies a centrifugal force on the astronaut
questions.solution:
1. On Earth, the weight of the astronaut is

    $$W = mg = 600\text{ N}\quad\bigl(m=\tfrac{W}{g}\bigr).$$

  2. In the International Space Station (ISS) both the station and the astronaut are in continuous free fall around the Earth. They have the same centripetal acceleration towards Earth.

  3. “Weightlessness” means the astronaut feels no contact force from the floor. In free fall:

    – Gravitational force $mg$ acts on the astronaut.

    – There is no normal reaction $N$ from the floor (because both fall together).

  4. Therefore:

    Option 2 is correct – the normal reaction of the space-station floor on the astronaut is zero.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: For an electric circuit given below, the correct combination of voltage (V) and current (I) is
questions.solution:
CONCEPT:

Parallel Circuits and Ohm’s Law

When resistors are in parallel, the voltage is the same across all resistors.

To find the total current, first combine all resistors into a single "equivalent resistance." Then use Ohm’s Law: V = IR or I = V / R.

EXPLANATION:

Step 1: Find Equivalent Resistance

The resistors, 100 Ω, 200 Ω, and 300 Ω, are all connected in parallel.

The formula for parallel resistors: 1/Req = 1/100 + 1/200 + 1/300

Let’s add:
1/100 = 6/600
1/200 = 3/600
1/300 = 2/600

Add together: (6 + 3 + 2) / 600 = 11 / 600

So, Req = 600 / 11 ≈ 54.55 Ω

Step 2: Find the Voltage

Ohm’s Law says V = IR.

From the circuit, the current through the 100 Ω resistor is given as 3 A.

So, V = 100 × 3 = 300 V

Step 3: Find the Total Current

Now, use the voltage (300 V) to find the current through each resistor:

  • For 100 Ω: I = 300 / 100 = 3 A
  • For 200 Ω: I = 300 / 200 = 1.5 A
  • For 300 Ω: I = 300 / 300 = 1 A

Total current = 3 + 1.5 + 1 = 5.5 A

So, the correct answer is: Option 2) V = 300 V ; I = 5.5 A

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: The work done by the force acting on an object is zero if the displacement of the object
options: is in the opposite direction of the direction of force
is in the same direction of the direction of force
is in perpendicular direction of the direction of force
questions.solution:
Work done is given by

$$ W = F s \cos \theta $$

where:

  • $F$ = force

  • $s$ = displacement

  • $\theta$ = angle between force and displacement

For work done to be zero,

$$ \cos \theta = 0 $$

This happens when

$$ \theta = 90^\circ $$

That means the displacement is perpendicular to the direction of force.

So, the correct answer is:

Option 3: is in perpendicular direction of the direction of force

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText:
Which one of the following statements regarding simple pendulum is correct?

Simple pendulum has a time period independent of amplitude:
options: only for small amplitudes because then the net force on its bob is independent of its displacement.
for any amplitude because the net force on the bob is always proportional to its displacement.
for any amplitude because the net force on the bob is independent of its displacement.
only for small amplitudes because then the net force on its bob is proportional to its displacement.
questions.solution:
In order to accurately describe the behavior of a simple pendulum, we need to analyze its physics in detail. A simple pendulum consists of a mass (known as the bob) attached to a string of length $ L $ and fixed at a pivot point. When displaced from its equilibrium position and released, it oscillates back and forth under the influence of gravity.

The restoring force acting on the bob is provided by the component of the gravitational force that acts along the direction of the arc. This force can be expressed as:

$$ F = -mg \sin(\theta) $$

where:

  • F is the restoring force,
  • m is the mass of the bob,
  • g is the acceleration due to gravity,
  • θ is the angular displacement from the vertical.

For small angular displacements (small amplitudes), $ \sin(\theta) $ can be approximated by $ \theta $ (when $ \theta $ is measured in radians). Thus, the restoring force becomes:

$$ F \approx -mg\theta $$

Since $ \theta = \frac{x}{L} $ where $ x $ is the linear displacement and $ L $ is the length of the pendulum, the force can be rewritten as:

$$ F \approx -\frac{mg}{L} x $$

This shows that the restoring force is directly proportional to the displacement, characteristic of simple harmonic motion. The time period $ T $ of a simple pendulum is then given by:

$$ T = 2\pi \sqrt{\frac{L}{g}} $$

Note that this time period is independent of amplitude, but the approximation $ \sin(\theta) \approx \theta $ only holds for small amplitudes. Therefore, the correct statement regarding the simple pendulum is:

Option D: only for small amplitudes because then the net force on its bob is proportional to its displacement.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements for the work done by gravity on a body is NOT correct ?
options: Work done is independent of the path followed by the body
Work done depends only on the vertical distance separating the initial and the final position of the body
Work done depends on the path followed by the body
Work done is zero if the body is displaced horizontally to the force of gravity
questions.solution:
The incorrect statement is:

Option 3: Work done depends on the path followed by the body

Step-by-step explanation

Gravity is a conservative force. For a conservative force:

  • Work done does not depend on the path

  • It depends only on the initial and final positions

For gravity, work done is given by:

$$ W = mg(h_1 - h_2) $$

where:

  • $m$ = mass of the body

  • $g$ = acceleration due to gravity

  • $h_1$ = initial height

  • $h_2$ = final height

So:

  • If only the vertical height changes, gravity does work.

  • If the displacement is purely horizontal, then there is no change in height, so work done by gravity is zero.

Checking each option

  1. Work done is independent of the path followed by the body

    This is correct.

  2. Work done depends only on the vertical distance separating the initial and the final position of the body

    This is correct.

  3. Work done depends on the path followed by the body

    This is not correct.

  4. Work done is zero if the body is displaced horizontally to the force of gravity

    This is correct.

Final answer

$$ \boxed{\text{Option 3}} $$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Two resistances of 5.0 Ω and 7.0 Ω are connected in series and the combination is connected in parallel with a resistance of 36.0 Ω. The equivalent resistance of the combination of three resistors is
questions.solution:
Step 1: Write down what is given.

  • Two resistances $R_1 = 5.0 \, \Omega$ and $R_2 = 7.0 \, \Omega$ are connected in series.

  • Their series combination is connected in parallel with $R_3 = 36.0 \, \Omega$.

Step 2: Find the series resistance of $R_1$ and $R_2$.

$ R_{\text{series}} = R_1 + R_2 = 5.0 + 7.0 = 12.0 \, \Omega $

Step 3: Now, $R_{\text{series}} = 12.0 \, \Omega$ is in parallel with $R_3 = 36.0 \, \Omega$.

For two resistors in parallel:

$ \frac{1}{R_{\text{eq}}} = \frac{1}{R_{\text{series}}} + \frac{1}{R_3} $

$ \frac{1}{R_{\text{eq}}} = \frac{1}{12} + \frac{1}{36} $

$ \frac{1}{R_{\text{eq}}} = \frac{3 + 1}{36} = \frac{4}{36} = \frac{1}{9} $

Step 4: Take reciprocal to get the equivalent resistance.

$ R_{\text{eq}} = 9.0 \, \Omega $

Final Answer: Option 3 → 9.0 Ω

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: SONAR stands for
questions.solution:
The correct answer is:

Option 3: Sound Navigation and Ranging

SONAR is a technique that uses sound waves to detect and locate objects under water.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: A microscope may be a combination of :
options: a convex lens and a convex mirror.
questions.solution:
A microscope is typically a combination of two convex lenses. The basic design of a simple microscope, also known as a magnifying glass, uses a single convex lens. However, compound microscopes, which are more common in scientific applications, use two convex lenses arranged in series to achieve higher magnifying power.

Each lens in a compound microscope serves a specific purpose:

Objective Lens: This is the lens closest to the object being viewed. It gathers light from the specimen and creates a enlarged real image.

Eyepiece Lens (Ocular Lens): This lens is closest to the observer's eye. It magnifies the real image created by the objective lens to create a larger virtual image that the observer can see.

The arrangement of these two lenses allows for greater magnification and resolution compared to a single lens. Therefore, the correct option is:

Option A: two convex lenses.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: If the current through an electrical machine running on direct current is $15 A$ and the machine runs for $10$ minutes, the charge that passes through the machine during this time is:
questions.solution:
To determine the charge that passes through the machine, we use the relationship between current, charge, and time. The formula that relates these quantities is given by :

$$ Q = I \times t $$

where :

$$ Q $$ is the charge (in coulombs, C)

$$ I $$ is the current (in amperes, A)

$$ t $$ is the time (in seconds, s)

We are given :

Current, $$ I = 15 \, \text{A} $$

Time, $$ t = 10 \, \text{minutes} $$

First, we need to convert the time from minutes to seconds :

$$ 10 \, \text{minutes} \times 60 \, \text{seconds per minute} = 600 \, \text{seconds} $$

Now, substituting the given values into the formula, we get :

$$ Q = 15 \, \text{A} \times 600 \, \text{s} $$

$$ Q = 9000 \, \text{C} $$

Therefore, the charge that passes through the machine during this time is :

Option D : $$ 9000 \, \text{C} $$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one among the following diagrams may correctly represent the motion of a skydiver during a jump ?
questions.solution:
CONCEPT:

How a Skydiver Falls

Step 1: Skydiver Jumps

When a skydiver jumps out, gravity pulls them down. Their speed goes up fast at first because they are falling freely through the air.

Step 2: Reaching Terminal Velocity

As the skydiver falls faster, air pushes back harder. Eventually, the push from the air (air resistance) matches the pull of gravity, so the skydiver's speed stops increasing. This steady speed is called terminal velocity.

Step 3: Opening the Parachute

When the parachute opens, there is a sudden big increase in air resistance. This makes the skydiver slow down very quickly.

Speed-Time Graph:

The speed-time graph starts by going up quickly (as the skydiver speeds up). Then, it flattens out (terminal velocity). After the parachute opens, the line drops sharply as the skydiver slows down fast.

EXPLANATION:

Option 1:

This graph is correct. It shows speed going up fast, staying the same for a while (terminal velocity), and then dropping quickly after the parachute opens.

Option 2:

This option is incorrect because it makes the speed go to zero too fast, which doesn't happen in real skydiving.

Option 3:

This option is wrong because it shows speed going up and down in a straight line, as if the skydiver stops suddenly without a parachute. This is unrealistic.

Option 4:

This option almost matches what really happens, but it does not clearly show a flat part for terminal velocity and the slowing down isn't sudden enough when the parachute opens.

So, Option 1 is correct.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Suppose, a ball of mass M is thrown upwards from a point A and it reaches up to the highest point B and returns back to point A, which one among the following is correct ?
options: Kinetic Energy at A = Potential Energy at B
Kinetic Energy at A = Potential Energy at A
Kinetic Energy at B = Potential Energy at B
Kinetic Energy at B = Kinetic Energy at A
questions.solution:
The correct option is:

$$\boxed{\text{Option 1: Kinetic Energy at A = Potential Energy at B}}$$

Step-by-step explanation:

Let the ball be thrown upward from point $A$ with initial speed $u$.

At point $A$:

  • The ball has kinetic energy

$$KE_A = \frac{1}{2}Mu^2$$

  • If we take point $A$ as the reference level, then

$$PE_A = 0$$

At highest point $B$:

  • The velocity becomes zero, so

$$KE_B = 0$$

  • Let the height reached be $h$, then potential energy at $B$ is

$$PE_B = Mgh$$

Now, by conservation of mechanical energy:

$$\frac{1}{2}Mu^2 = Mgh$$

So,

$$KE_A = PE_B$$

Hence, Option 1 is correct.

Why other options are wrong:

  • Option 2: At point $A$, potential energy is usually taken as zero, so it is not equal to kinetic energy.

  • Option 3: At point $B$, kinetic energy is zero but potential energy is not zero.

  • Option 4: At point $B$, kinetic energy is zero, but at $A$ it is maximum.

$$\boxed{\text{Correct Answer: Option 1}}$$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one among the following is correct for a person suffering from myopia?
options: The person can see near objects clearly
The person can see distant objects clearly
The person can neither see near objects nor distant objects clearly
questions.solution:
1. Myopia (short-sightedness) means the eye’s optical power is too strong or the eyeball is too long.

  2. Parallel rays from a distant object focus in front of the retina, so distant objects appear blurred.

  3. Rays from a nearby object diverge more and are brought to focus on the retina, so near objects appear clear.

Correct option: 1. The person can see near objects clearly.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: The magnetic field inside a current carrying very long solenoid is
questions.solution:
The correct answer is:

Option 1: Uniform

For a very long current-carrying solenoid, the magnetic field inside is nearly the same at all points inside it.

According to NCERT, the magnetic field lines inside a long solenoid are:

  • parallel

  • equally spaced

  • directed along the axis of the solenoid

This shows that the magnetic field inside is uniform.

So, the answer is:

$$\boxed{\text{Uniform}}$$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: What will happen if a collection of positive and negative charges are passed at a high speed through a magnetic field which is perpendicular to the direction of motion of the charges? (Assume that both kind of charges are NOT going to recombine)
options: Both kind of charges will stop moving
Positive charges and negative charges will separate out
Positive charges will stop but negative charges will continue moving uninterrupted
Both kind of charges will keep moving uninterrupted
questions.solution:
When a collection of positive and negative charges is passed at high speed through a magnetic field that is perpendicular to the direction of motion of the charges, the charges experience the Lorentz force, which acts perpendicular to both their velocity and the magnetic field.

Lorentz Force

The force experienced by a charged particle in a magnetic field is given by:

$ \mathbf{F} = q (\mathbf{v} \times \mathbf{B}) $

where:

  • $ \mathbf{F} $ is the force on the charge

  • $ q $ is the charge of the particle

  • $ \mathbf{v} $ is the velocity of the particle

  • $ \mathbf{B} $ is the magnetic field

Effects of Lorentz Force on Charges


  1. Positive charges: They will experience a force in a direction given by the right-hand rule, causing them to curve in one direction.

  2. Negative charges: They will experience a force in the opposite direction to the positive charges (due to the negative sign of their charge), causing them to curve in the opposite direction.

Key Points:


  • Both positive and negative charges experience the Lorentz force.

  • The Lorentz force will indeed cause the charges to move in curved paths rather than straight lines.

  • Despite the curved paths, the charges will continue to move unless there is some other external force or condition that stops them.

  • The problem states that both types of charges are not going to recombine, meaning they will continue moving in their respective curved paths due to the Lorentz force.

Conclusion:

Both kinds of charges will keep moving, albeit in curved paths due to the Lorentz force acting on them. This does not mean they will move uninterrupted in straight lines, but they will not stop or separate based on the information given.

Therefore, the correct interpretation aligns with the explanation:

Option D: Both kinds of charges will keep moving uninterrupted.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following wavelength radiations may correspond to an X-ray ?
questions.solution:
X-rays have very short wavelengths, usually in the range of about

$$ 0.01 \text{ nm to } 10 \text{ nm} $$

Now check the options:

  • $1 \text{ nm}$: lies in the X-ray range

  • $10 \text{ nm}$: at the extreme end, but usually not taken as the standard answer here

  • $100 \text{ nm}$: ultraviolet region

  • $300 \text{ nm}$: ultraviolet region

So, the correct answer is:

$$ \boxed{1 \text{ nm}} $$

Option 1.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements is true for sound waves propa- gating in air?
options: Sound is an electromagnetic wave and transverse in nature
Sound is a mechanical wave and longitudinal in nature
Sound is a mechanical wave and transverse in nature
Sound is an electromagnetic wave and longitudinal in nature
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option B: Sound is a mechanical wave and longitudinal in nature.

Here's why:

Mechanical Waves:

  • Mechanical waves require a medium to travel. They are disturbances that travel through a medium by transferring energy from one particle to the next.
  • Sound waves are mechanical because they need air, water, solids, or any other medium to propagate.

Longitudinal Waves:

  • In longitudinal waves, the particles of the medium vibrate parallel to the direction of wave propagation. This means that the particles move back and forth in the same direction that the wave is traveling.
  • Sound waves are longitudinal because the air molecules vibrate back and forth in the same direction that the sound wave is traveling.

Why other options are incorrect:

  • Option A and D: Electromagnetic waves do not require a medium to travel. They can travel through a vacuum, like space. Examples of electromagnetic waves include light, radio waves, and X-rays.
  • Option C: Transverse waves are waves where the particles of the medium vibrate perpendicular to the direction of wave propagation. An example of a transverse wave is a wave on a string.

In summary, sound waves are mechanical waves that travel through a medium and are longitudinal because the particles of the medium vibrate parallel to the direction of wave propagation.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: A body sinks in water when
options: density of water is greater than density of body
density of water is less than density of body
density of water is equal to density of body
questions.solution:
A body sinks in water when its density is greater than the density of water.

So, the correct answer is:

Option 2: density of water is less than density of body

Reason:

According to the NCERT concept of flotation:

  • If density of body $>$ density of liquid, the body sinks

  • If density of body $<$ density of liquid, the body floats

  • If density of body $=$ density of liquid, the body may remain suspended

Here, for sinking in water:

$$ \text{Density of body} > \text{Density of water} $$

which means

$$ \text{Density of water} < \text{Density of body} $$

Hence, Option 2 is correct.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: For a human eye, where u is the distance of an object from the eye, f is the focal length of the lens and v is the distance of image from the eye, which is the correct schematic graph?
questions.solution: When object distance increase, in order to maintain image at retina, focal length increases initially, later for extreme distance it becomes constant. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: The electric field lines from an isolated positively charged conducting sphere are
options: at right angles to the conducting surface and towards the centre of the sphere
at any angle to the conducting surface
at right angles to the conducting surface and outwards from the centre of the sphere

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following equations related to the motion of an object is NOT correct? (Symbols carry their usual meanings)
options: u = v − at
u2 − v2 = 2as
Distance traveled during the nth second = u + (1/2)a(2n - 1)
questions.solution:
The equation that’s NOT correct is Option 3. The standard “third” equation of motion is

$$v^2 = u^2 + 2as$$

which can be rearranged as

$$u^2 - v^2 = -\,2as$$

—not $$u^2 - v^2 = 2as\,. $$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: A point object is placed at the centre of curvature of a spherical concave mirror. Which one among the fol- lowing would be the correct location of image formed?
options: Between the focal point and the centre of curvature
questions.solution:
1. For a concave mirror of radius of curvature $R$, the focal length is

    $$f=\frac{R}{2}$$

  2. Mirror formula (Cartesian sign convention):

    $$\frac{1}{f}=\frac{1}{v}+\frac{1}{u}$$

    where

    • $u$ = object distance from pole (real object ⇒ $u>0$)

    • $v$ = image distance from pole (real image ⇒ $v>0$)

  3. Here the object is at the centre of curvature, so

    $$u=R$$

  4. Substitute into the mirror formula:

    $$\frac{1}{R/2}=\frac{1}{v}+\frac{1}{R}$$

    $$\frac{2}{R}=\frac{1}{v}+\frac{1}{R}$$

    $$\frac{1}{v}=\frac{2}{R}-\frac{1}{R}=\frac{1}{R}$$

    $$\therefore v=R$$

  5. Hence the image is formed at a distance $R$ from the pole, i.e. at the centre of curvature.

Answer: Option 2 – At the centre of curvature.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following sketches correctly describes a lever of second class?
questions.solution:
CONCEPT:

Lever of Second Class

A lever of the second class has three main parts:

  • The fulcrum is at one end. The fulcrum is the point where the lever pivots or turns.
  • The load (the thing you want to move or lift) is in the middle.
  • The effort (where you push or pull) is at the other end, opposite the fulcrum.

In a second-class lever:

  • The load is always between the fulcrum and where you apply effort.
  • The arm where you apply effort (effort arm) is always longer than the part holding the load (load arm), so you get a mechanical advantage greater than 1. This means it's easier to lift something.
  • Examples of second-class levers are wheelbarrows, nutcrackers, and bottle openers.

EXPLANATION:

When you look at sketches of different levers:

  • A second-class lever should always have:
    • Fulcrum at one end,
    • Load in the middle,
    • And effort at the other end.
  • The correct sketch must show the load between the fulcrum and the effort.

So, the sketch that correctly shows a lever of the second class is Option 2.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Liquid vegetable oils are converted to solid margarine by the use of
questions.solution:
The process of converting liquid vegetable oils into solid margarine is known as hydrogenation. During hydrogenation, hydrogen gas is used to saturate the carbon atoms in the fatty acids of the vegetable oils. This addition of hydrogen atoms transforms the unsaturated fats (which are typically liquid at room temperature) into saturated fats (which are solid at room temperature). Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option A: hydrogen gas

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: What is the coordination number of Na⁺ and Cl⁻ ions in NaCl lattice?
questions.solution:
The coordination number in a crystal lattice refers to the number of nearest neighbor atoms or ions surrounding a particular atom or ion. In the case of sodium chloride (NaCl) lattice, both the Na⁺ ions and Cl⁻ ions are arranged in a face-centered cubic (FCC) structure. This structure is commonly known as a rock-salt structure.

In the NaCl lattice, each Na⁺ ion is surrounded by 6 Cl⁻ ions, and similarly, each Cl⁻ ion is surrounded by 6 Na⁺ ions. This configuration ensures the stability of the ionic crystal by maximizing the attractive electrostatic forces between the oppositely charged ions while minimizing repulsive forces.

Therefore, the coordination number of Na⁺ ions is 6 and the coordination number of Cl⁻ ions is also 6.

Thus, the correct option is Option C:

6, 6

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which among the following is the correct composition of Borax?
questions.solution:
Borax, also known as sodium borate, is a mineral and a salt of boric acid. Its chemical composition includes sodium (Na), boron (B), oxygen (O), and hydrogen (H). The correct formula for Borax is $$\text{Na}_2\text{B}_4\text{O}_7\cdot10\text{H}_2\text{O}$$.

Given the options, the correct composition of Borax is:

Option B

Sodium, Boron, Oxygen and Hydrogen

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: The mass number of argon is 40. Which one of the following statements is correct?
options: The number of protons in argon is 22.
The number of neutrons in argon is 18.
The number of electrons in argon is 18.
The sum of numbers of protons and electrons in argon is 40.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is NOT present in cement ?
options: 3CaO.SiO2
4CaO.SiO2
questions.solution:
The correct answer is:

$$\boxed{4CaO \cdot SiO_2}$$

So, Option 3 is NOT present in cement.

Explanation:

The main constituents of cement are:

  1. Tricalcium silicate

    $$ 3CaO \cdot SiO_2 $$

  2. Dicalcium silicate

    $$ 2CaO \cdot SiO_2 $$

  3. Tricalcium aluminate

    $$ 3CaO \cdot Al_2O_3 $$

  4. Tetracalcium aluminoferrite

    $$ 4CaO \cdot Al_2O_3 \cdot Fe_2O_3 $$

Among the given options,

$$ 4CaO \cdot SiO_2 $$

is not a normal constituent of cement.

Final Answer:

$$\boxed{\text{Option 3}}$$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements regarding oxidation and reduction reactions is NOT correct?
options: If a substance loses hydrogen during reaction, it is reduced
If a substance loses oxygen during reaction, it is reduced
If a substance gains hydrogen during reaction, it is reduced
If a substance gains oxygen during reaction, it is oxidized
questions.solution:
The statement that’s NOT correct is Option 1.

Here's why:

Oxidation involves either gaining O₂ or losing H₂ (loss of electrons).

Reduction involves either losing O₂ or gaining H₂ (gain of electrons).

So losing hydrogen is an oxidation process, not a reduction.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: The intermixing of particles of two different types of matter on their own is called
questions.solution:
Option 3: Diffusion

  1. Definition

    • Diffusion is the spontaneous movement and intermixing of particles of two different substances from a region of higher concentration to a region of lower concentration, without any external force.

  2. Why not the others

    • Osmosis – diffusion of solvent (usually water) through a semipermeable membrane, not general intermixing.

    • Brownian motion – random zig-zag movement of colloidal particles in a fluid, caused by collisions with molecules; does not itself describe mixing of two substances.

    • Conductance – ability of a material to conduct electricity, unrelated to particle mixing.

  3. Key point

    In diffusion, particles move down their concentration gradient until uniform distribution is reached.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is the correct molecular formula of ammonium carbonate if the valency of ammonium ion is (+1) and carbonate anion is (-2)?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which among the following is NOT true with respect to colloidal solution?
options: Particles are uniformly distributed throughout the solution
Colloidal solution is homogenous in nature
They do not settle down when kept undisturbed
questions.solution:
To determine which option is not true with respect to colloidal solutions, let's examine each statement in detail:

Option A: Particles are uniformly distributed throughout the solution

This statement is true. In a colloidal solution, the particles, known as the dispersed phase, are distributed uniformly throughout the dispersing medium. This uniform distribution is a key characteristic of colloidal solutions.

Option B: Colloidal solution is homogenous in nature

This statement is not true. Although colloidal solutions appear homogeneous to the naked eye, they are actually heterogeneous in nature. The dispersed particles are microscopically dispersed throughout the medium, which causes deviations from true homogeneity when observed under a microscope.

Option C: They show Tyndall effect

This statement is true. Colloidal solutions exhibit the Tyndall effect, which is the scattering of light by colloidal particles. This scattering makes a beam of light visible when it passes through the colloidal solution.

Option D: They do not settle down when kept undisturbed

This statement is true. In a colloidal solution, the particles are small enough that they do not settle down due to gravity. Instead, they remain in a dispersed state, preventing sedimentation.

Based on the above analysis, the statement that is NOT true with respect to colloidal solutions is:

Option B: Colloidal solution is homogenous in nature

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: The number of structural isomers of pentane is
questions.solution:
The correct answer is 3.

Isomers:

  • Chemical compounds that have identical chemical formulae but differ in properties and the arrangement of atoms in the molecule are called isomers.
    • Therefore, the compounds that exhibit isomerism are known as isomers.
  • Such structural isomers, which differ in the chain of carbon atoms, are known as chain isomers.

Pentane:

  • Pentane is an organic compound that is a clear, colourless liquid.
  • The odour of pentane is similar to petroleum.
  • It is less dense than water and is insoluble in water. It is also known as quintane and n-pentane.
  • The rotatable bond count of pentane is 2.
  • Molecular Formula: C5H12.

Isomers of Pentane: There are three structural isomers of pentane:

  • n-pentane is the first isomer, which is a straight chain with a normal structure of pentane.

  • 2-methylbutane is the second isomer, which is a branched-chain structure in which one carbon atom is connected to three other carbon atoms.

  • 2,2-dimethylpropane is the third isomer, which is a branched-chain structure with the central carbon atom connected to four other carbon atoms.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: The elemental composition of diamond is
questions.solution: Diamond is actually a fascinating material because it's an allotrope of carbon. This means it's made up entirely of carbon atoms, but they're arranged in a very specific, strong crystal structure. This unique arrangement of carbon atoms is what gives diamond its incredible hardness and brilliance!

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Bee sting leaves an acid which causes pain and irritation. The acid released is
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option B: methanoic acid.

Here's why:

  • Methanoic acid (HCOOH), also known as formic acid, is the simplest carboxylic acid. It's the primary irritant in bee stings (and ant stings too!).

  • Tartaric acid is found in grapes and is used in baking.

  • Ethanoic acid is found in vinegar.

  • Citric acid is found in citrus fruits like lemons and oranges.

The pain and irritation you feel after a bee sting are due to the methanoic acid interacting with your body's cells, causing inflammation and a pain response.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Common salt (NaCl) is not used as a raw material for preparation of which one of the following compounds?
questions.solution:
Common salt (NaCl) is not used as a raw material for the preparation of Plaster of Paris.

Plaster of Paris is chemically calcium sulfate hemihydrate, with the formula $$ \text{CaSO}_4 \cdot \frac{1}{2}\text{H}_2\text{O} $$, and is produced from gypsum ($\text{CaSO}_4 \cdot 2\text{H}_2\text{O}$) through a heating process.

Here's a quick explanation of whether and how NaCl is involved in the production of the other compounds:

Bleaching Powder: It is prepared using calcium hydroxide ($$\text{Ca(OH)}_2$$) and chlorine gas, which can be derived from the electrolysis of NaCl solution.

Baking Soda: Sodium bicarbonate ($$\text{NaHCO}_3$$) is made using the Solvay process, which involves NaCl, ammonia, and carbon dioxide.

Washing Soda: Sodium carbonate ($$\text{Na}_2\text{CO}_3$$) is also produced via the Solvay process, using NaCl as an essential raw material.

Therefore, the correct answer is Option C: Plaster of Paris.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following type of medicines is used for treating indigestion ?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is:

Option 4: Antacid

Explanation:

Indigestion is usually caused by excess acid in the stomach. Antacids are medicines that help to neutralise the extra acid and give relief from indigestion.

Other options:

  • Analgesic: used to relieve pain

  • Antiseptic: used to prevent infection in wounds

  • Antibiotic: used to kill or stop the growth of bacteria

So, the medicine used for treating indigestion is Antacid.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: The chemical reaction : 2AgCl (s) → 2Ag (s) + Cl<sub>2</sub> (g) takes place
questions.solution:
1. Silver chloride, AgCl, is a photosensitive compound.

  2. Under sunlight, AgCl absorbs photons. This provides the energy needed to break the Ag–Cl bonds.

  3. The photochemical decomposition is represented as:

    $$2AgCl(s)\xrightarrow{\text{sunlight}}2Ag(s)+Cl_2(g)$$

  4. In the dark, on ordinary heating or under pressure, this reaction does not proceed appreciably.

Answer: Option 2 – in sunlight.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: An iron nail dipped in copper sulphate solution turns brown. This is due to which one of the following types of reactions?
questions.solution:
The iron nail turning brown when dipped in copper sulphate solution is due to the following reaction:

$$ \text{Fe (s)} + \text{CuSO}_4 \text{(aq)} \rightarrow \text{FeSO}_4 \text{(aq)} + \text{Cu (s)} $$

In this reaction, iron displaces copper from copper sulphate solution, forming iron sulphate and depositing copper metal on the nail, which is observed as a brown layer.

Thus, the correct type of reaction is:

Option D: Displacement reaction

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is a covalent compound?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Mica is mainly used in:
questions.solution:
The correct answer is:

Option D: electrical and electronic industries.

Mica is predominantly used in the electrical and electronic industries due to its excellent insulating properties and high dielectric strength. It is commonly found in components such as capacitors, insulators, and various other electrical equipment.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: At which temperature does liquid water show maximum density?
questions.solution:
Liquid water reaches its maximum density at about 4 °C, which is 277 K.

• Answer: Option 2 (277 K)

• Reason: Below 4 °C, the increasing prominence of hydrogen-bond–driven open structures makes water expand, so its density peaks right at 4 °C.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which among the following is the popular method for manufacture of ammonia?
questions.solution:
The popular method for the manufacture of ammonia is:

Option B: Haber-Bosch process

The Haber-Bosch process is widely used industrially to synthesize ammonia ($$\text{NH}_3$$) from nitrogen ($$\text{N}_2$$) and hydrogen ($$\text{H}_2$$) gases. Here is a brief description:

Reaction: $$ \text{N}_2(g) + 3\text{H}_2(g) \rightarrow 2\text{NH}_3(g) $$

Conditions: Typically conducted at high pressures (150-200 atm) and high temperatures (400-500 °C) with an iron catalyst.

This process was developed by Fritz Haber and Carl Bosch, and it revolutionized agriculture by providing a method to produce large quantities of ammonia, which is a key ingredient in fertilizers.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Airbags work on the principle of a chemical reaction triggered by the impact producing a gaseous product that causes a sudden volume change. Which one among the following chemical conversions is responsible for this?
options: Solid carbon dioxide into gaseous carbon dioxide
Sudden conversion of gaseous carbon dioxide into carbon monoxide
questions.solution:
The correct answer is:

Option A: Sodium azide into nitrogen gas

Airbags typically use the rapid decomposition of sodium azide (NaN₃) to produce nitrogen gas (N₂), which inflates the airbag. The chemical reaction involved is:

$$ 2 \text{NaN}_3 \rightarrow 2 \text{Na} + 3 \text{N}_2 \uparrow $$

This reaction produces a large volume of nitrogen gas very quickly, causing the airbag to inflate.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Buckminster fullerene, which looks like a football, is an allotropic form of
questions.solution:
Buckminster fullerene, also known as C60, is a spherical molecule made entirely of carbon atoms arranged in a pattern that resembles a soccer ball (football). The structure consists of 60 carbon atoms forming 12 pentagons and 20 hexagons, creating a highly stable and symmetrical shape. This particular molecular form of carbon falls under the group of compounds known as fullerenes, which are allotropes of carbon.

In chemistry, allotropes are different structural forms of the same element, where atoms are bonded together in different ways. For carbon, other notable allotropes include diamond, graphite, and graphene. Buckminster fullerene is an exciting form due to its unique properties and potential applications in materials science and nanotechnology.

Given the options:

Option A: Phosphorus

Option B: Sulfur

Option C: Carbon

Option D: Tin

The correct answer is Option C: Carbon.

Thus, Buckminster fullerene is an allotropic form of carbon.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: The temperature at which “vapour pressure of the liquid in an open vessel becomes equal to the atmospheric pressure” is called
questions.solution:
The correct answer is:

Option 2: Boiling point

Explanation:

According to NCERT, boiling point is the temperature at which the vapour pressure of a liquid becomes equal to the atmospheric pressure.

At this temperature, the liquid starts changing into vapour throughout the liquid.

So,

$$ \text{Boiling point} = \text{temperature where vapour pressure} = \text{atmospheric pressure} $$

Answer: Boiling point

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Water gas is a mixture of:
questions.solution:
Water gas is a mixture of carbon monoxide (CO) and hydrogen (H₂). It is produced by passing steam over hot carbon (coke) at high temperatures, typically around 1000°C. The chemical reaction for the formation of water gas is as follows:

$$\text{C} + \text{H}_2\text{O} \rightarrow \text{CO} + \text{H}_2$$

Given this information, the correct option is:

Option B: CO and H₂
